items.prompt: Plot the point `(3, -2)` and explain what each coordinate controls.
items.expected_evidence: interpret approaching behavior consistently across tables, graphs, formulas, and verbal descriptions
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates interpret approaching behavior consistently across tables, graphs, formulas, and verbal descriptions with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Find the slope between `(1, 4)` and `(5, 12)` and interpret it as a rate of change.
items.expected_evidence: distinguish function value, limit value, and continuity status in bounded cases
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates distinguish function value, limit value, and continuity status in bounded cases with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Determine whether the table represents a function and justify your decision.
items.expected_evidence: classify bounded discontinuity and asymptotic behavior using correct limit language
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates classify bounded discontinuity and asymptotic behavior using correct limit language with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Explain how a table, graph, and equation can represent the same linear relationship.
items.expected_evidence: interpret approaching behavior consistently across tables, graphs, formulas, and verbal descriptions
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to interpret approaching behavior consistently across tables, graphs, formulas, and verbal descriptions.

items.prompt: Explain what slope means in both graphical and contextual terms.
items.expected_evidence: distinguish function value, limit value, and continuity status in bounded cases
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to distinguish function value, limit value, and continuity status in bounded cases.

items.prompt: A student reverses the coordinates and plots `(3, -2)` as `(-2, 3)`. Diagnose the mapping error. Focus on limit process static view.
items.expected_evidence: interpret approaching behavior consistently across tables, graphs, formulas, and verbal descriptions
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward interpret approaching behavior consistently across tables, graphs, formulas, and verbal descriptions.

items.prompt: A student says every straight-looking graph is proportional. Identify the misclassification. Focus on derivative meaning fragmentation.
items.expected_evidence: distinguish function value, limit value, and continuity status in bounded cases
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward distinguish function value, limit value, and continuity status in bounded cases.

items.prompt: A ride-share charges a fixed fee plus a cost per mile. Write a rule, make a table, and interpret the slope and intercept.
items.expected_evidence: interpret approaching behavior consistently across tables, graphs, formulas, and verbal descriptions
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves interpret approaching behavior consistently across tables, graphs, formulas, and verbal descriptions in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: Given a graph and a verbal description, decide whether they could represent the same relationship and justify the match.
items.expected_evidence: distinguish function value, limit value, and continuity status in bounded cases
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves distinguish function value, limit value, and continuity status in bounded cases in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: On a delayed check, move between a rule, a table, and a graph for a linear relationship and explain the connection.
items.expected_evidence: interpret approaching behavior consistently across tables, graphs, formulas, and verbal descriptions
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner still demonstrates interpret approaching behavior consistently across tables, graphs, formulas, and verbal descriptions on a delayed recheck without a blocking recurrence.

items.prompt: Use a prerequisite probe on precalculus function synthesis and limits foundations to see whether the learner is blocked by graph/coordinate foundations or by limits and continuity itself.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether precalculus function synthesis and limits foundations is secure enough to support limits and continuity
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in precalculus function synthesis and limits foundations from a current-node issue in limits and continuity.
items.suspected_knowledge_gap_if_incorrect: precalculus function synthesis and limits foundations

items.prompt: Use a prerequisite probe on function as input output rule to see whether the learner is blocked by graph/coordinate foundations or by limits and continuity itself.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether function as input output rule is secure enough to support limits and continuity
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in function as input output rule from a current-node issue in limits and continuity.
